title: Backwards-incompatible change between 4.0.2 and 4.1.1?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

My podman version was recently upgraded from (what I assume was) 4.0.2 to 4.1.1, after said upgrade my containers stopped working with the following error:

`Error: invalid config provided: extra host entries must be specified on the pod: network cannot be configured when it is shared with a pod`

I assume that this is due to `podman-compose` version 0.1.x using the `--add-host` flag for networking, this problem does not happen with at least `podman` version 4.0.2 (tested by rolling back package) nor with `podman-compose` version 1.x, as it uses a different networking system.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman 4.1.1

2. Install podman-compose 0.1.x

3. Run any compose file that uses intra-container networking with `podman-compose`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman run --name=foo -d --pod=foo --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=foo --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.project=foo --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/foo --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml,docker-compose.test.yml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=foo -e POSTGRESQL_DATABASE=foo -e POSTGRESQL_USER=foo -e POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD=foo -e PGPASSWORD=foo -e POSTGRESQL_HOST=foo -v foo-data:/var/lib/pgsql/data/ --add-host bar:127.0.0.1 --add-host baz:127.0.0.1 --add-host foo:127.0.0.1 --add-host foo:127.0.0.1 --add-host quux:127.0.0.1 --hostname foo registry.redhat.io/rhel8/postgresql-13:1 ./scripts/setup-foo.sh
Error: invalid config provided: extra host entries must be specified on the pod: network cannot be configured when it is shared with a pod
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
podman run --name=foo -d --pod=foo --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=foo --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.project=foo --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/foo --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml,docker-compose.test.yml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=foo -e POSTGRESQL_DATABASE=foo -e POSTGRESQL_USER=foo -e POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD=foo -e PGPASSWORD=foo -e POSTGRESQL_HOST=foo -v foo-data:/var/lib/pgsql/data/ --add-host bar:127.0.0.1 --add-host baz:127.0.0.1 --add-host foo:127.0.0.1 --add-host foo:127.0.0.1 --add-host quux:127.0.0.1 --hostname foo registry.redhat.io/rhel8/postgresql-13:1 ./scripts/setup-foo.sh
<container-id>
0
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Have uninstalled the relevant podman version so I don't have this info other than it was 4.1.1

**Output of `podman info`:**

Same as above

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Same as above

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Have not checked the latest version as it's not available as a package in my distribution (RHEL 8.6 CSB)
I have checked the troubleshooting guide and didn't find much

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine

comments.body: This was a deliberate change to how networking was handled - I can't find the issue where we discussed it, but we made the change as a bugfix given that the existing behavior seemed to be broken. I'll try to find more details, but @Luap99 (who drove the change) may have more of an idea when he gets back from vacation.

I wasn't aware that this was used by podman-compose. The old podman behaviour was IMO terrible broken, since it would add the hosts for all containers in a pod since /etc/hosts is shared and also never deleted them. Thus restarting a container multiple times would add the same name over and over.

I understand that this was a breaking change but I see no good way around this. Also since 4.1 and 4.2 is out for a while now I think it is unlikely that we revert this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing, it's still an issue for me but an acceptable workaround exists (downgrade `podman` to `4.0.x` or upgrade `podman-compose` to `1.x`)
